annotation_policy: CMT and Koenigsbergian annotations derived from MIPVU-reviewed metaphorical and uncertain lexical units. Each instance cites at least one MIPVU ID with decision_type in METAPHOR_DECISIONS.

entailments: `Sieg` frames political, ideological, or racial victory as a natural-law outcome or quasi-agent in evolutionary-struggle rhetoric
Ideological or racial dominance is understood through military victory.
cmt.rival_reading: Battle-victory is extended to denote the triumph of races, ideologies, or laws of nature; the competitive combat source domain is applied to politics and biology.
cmt.justification: MIPVU review rule '' marked 1 unit(s) as metaphor-related or uncertain in this sentence.

instances.gloss_notes: German `Sieg` may be a literal battle outcome or a quasi-agent (der Sieg des Stärkeren = the victory of the stronger) in evolutionary-struggle framing.

entailments: `Held`/`Heldentum` frames soldiers' death or self-sacrifice as heroic idealization structured by the cultural hero-type
Soldier death and national sacrifice are understood through heroic idealization.
cmt.rival_reading: Individual soldiers or citizens are idealized through the hero category; heroism becomes the organizing concept for military self-sacrifice.
cmt.justification: MIPVU review rule '' marked 1 unit(s) as metaphor-related or uncertain in this sentence.

instances.gloss_notes: German `Held` in nationalist rhetoric may denote a literal soldier-hero or a culturally-idealized sacrificial figure.

entailments: `Geist` extends the notion of individual intellect or spirit to civilizational, racial, or cultural agency
Cultural or civilizational capacity is understood through collective spirit/mind.
cmt.rival_reading: Individual mind is extended to collective cultural/civilizational agency; abstract spirit becomes a force that builds or degrades civilizations.
cmt.justification: MIPVU review rule '' marked 1 unit(s) as metaphor-related or uncertain in this sentence.

instances.gloss_notes: German `Geist` spans intellectual capacity, cultural spirit, and spiritual agency; English spirit/mind/intellect each capture only part of the range.

entailments: `Seele` extends individual soul/psyche to the collective psyche of a people as something that can be won, poisoned, or protected
Collective political disposition is understood through individual soul as a capturable entity.
cmt.rival_reading: Individual soul is extended to collective psychological disposition; the collective soul becomes something that political actors can capture or poison.
cmt.justification: MIPVU review rule '' marked 1 unit(s) as metaphor-related or uncertain in this sentence.

instances.gloss_notes: German `Seele des Volkes` (soul of the people) extends the individual soul as a collective-organic metaphor.

entailments: `Vernichtung` extends physical destruction/annihilation to economic, political, or racial elimination of groups
Social or racial elimination is understood through total physical annihilation.
cmt.rival_reading: Physical destruction is extended to social/economic/racial elimination; the basic physical sense underwrites the metaphorical application to groups.
cmt.justification: MIPVU review rule '' marked 1 unit(s) as metaphor-related or uncertain in this sentence.

instances.gloss_notes: German `Vernichtung` denotes total destruction; in Nazi text often applied to enemies or states — may be literal or figurative depending on target.

entailments: `Sieg` frames political, ideological, or racial victory as a natural-law outcome or quasi-agent in evolutionary-struggle rhetoric
`Vernichtung` extends physical destruction/annihilation to economic, political, or racial elimination of groups
Ideological or racial dominance is understood through military victory.
cmt.rival_reading: Battle-victory is extended to denote the triumph of races, ideologies, or laws of nature; the competitive combat source domain is applied to politics and biology.
cmt.justification: MIPVU review rule '' marked 2 unit(s) as metaphor-related or uncertain in this sentence.

instances.gloss_notes: German `Sieg` may be a literal battle outcome or a quasi-agent (der Sieg des Stärkeren = the victory of the stronger) in evolutionary-struggle framing.

Collective political disposition is understood through individual soul as a capturable entity.
cmt.rival_reading: Individual soul is extended to collective psychological disposition; the collective soul becomes something that political actors can capture or poison.
cmt.justification: MIPVU review rule '' marked 1 unit(s) as metaphor-related or uncertain in this sentence.

entailments: `Blut` denotes racial lineage, genetic essence, or inherited cultural capacity — not literal blood in veins
Racial identity and cultural capacity are understood through the bodily blood domain.
cmt.rival_reading: Biological blood is extended to denote racial descent and inherited essence; the physical source domain underwrites racial-purity claims.
cmt.justification: MIPVU review rule '' marked 1 unit(s) as metaphor-related or uncertain in this sentence.

instances.gloss_notes: German `Blut` spans literal biological blood, racial-lineage metaphor, and sacrificial-cost rhetoric; context is decisive for MIPVU.
